response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with genuine developer-tooling and IDE-adjacent experience (LabWindows/CVI components, test runners, assertion libraries), but the core required stack is largely absent: no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise Java framework experience, and only peripheral Java work years ago. His depth is in D, TypeScript/Node.js, and web platforms rather than the JVM ecosystem, making him a poor match for a role centered on Java/Kotlin, Spring, and enterprise application servers despite his solid engineering craftsmanship.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI) and clear interest in language tooling
Built developer tools from scratch: test runners, assertion libraries, and web server libraries
Long track record with complex systems, including high-throughput low-latency services and strong TDD/craftsmanship practices
concerns: No Kotlin experience and no evident Spring or enterprise Java framework background
Java exposure is minor and dated, far short of the required deep JVM expertise
Recent career focus is TypeScript/Node.js product work, not JVM tooling or enterprise ecosystems
